title: Koeleria pauneroi
intro: Koeleria pauneroi, also known as Paunero's hair grass, is a species of perennial grass native to Argentina. It has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Koeleria pauneroi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Koeleria pauneroi is used to prepare teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Koeleria pauneroi is classified within the family Poaceae and is a species of grass. It belongs to the genus Koeleria. The genus Koeleria is a group of perennial grasses in the tribe Poeae. Koeleria pauneroi is further classified as a part of the subfamily Pooideae. The classification is based on its morphological characteristics and phylogenetic relationships.
